summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates SSH keys and pivots to LAN hosts
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads the installer's `/root/.ssh/id_rsa`, performs network reconnaissance (`ip addr`, `ip route`, `/proc/net/arp`), then SSHes with the stolen key to the default gateway and across the 172.17.0.0/24 subnet trying multiple usernames. It harvests `/readflag` output, `~/.npmrc` (both `/root/.npmrc` and `/home/node/.npmrc`), hostnames, and environment variables matching `HTB|FLAG`, then HTTP-PUTs the base64-wrapped output to a hardcoded attacker IP at `http://154.57.164.64:32271` (and a localhost mirror). The payload is gated by anti-sandbox checks: it skips execution under common CI environments and registry-mirror scanners (npmmirror, tencent, huawei, cnpm, taobao, ustc, etc.), and only detonates when the hostname matches a 12-hex Docker container ID pattern and cwd contains `/app`. The targeted detonation logic confirms malicious intent and is designed to evade defenders while compromising specific container environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
